assertions.expectation: Categorizes notifications by type: auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, end-of-life/deprecation, security patch
assertions.reason: Response provides a comprehensive classification matrix with all requested notification types: auto-upgrade scheduled, version available, end-of-life warnings, and security patches, plus additional relevant types like deprecated API usage and upgrade failures

assertions.expectation: Assigns urgency levels to each category (security patches = highest, new version available = lowest)
assertions.reason: Response assigns appropriate urgency levels in the classification matrix: security patches are Medium-High urgency, new version available is Low urgency, with proper gradation for other notification types

assertions.expectation: End-of-life warnings require action: plan upgrade before the support window closes
assertions.reason: Response clearly marks end-of-life warnings as High urgency with 'Required' action, provides detailed timeline-based criticality levels (90 days to 0 days), and includes specific action checklists for planning upgrades before EoS dates

assertions.expectation: Auto-upgrade scheduled notifications should trigger pre-upgrade checklist execution
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed triage questions checklist for auto-upgrade scheduled notifications, including checking upgrade type, business period impact, and team capacity, along with specific decision matrix for allow/defer/block actions

assertions.expectation: Security patch notifications may require expedited upgrades outside normal maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response addresses security patches with Medium-High urgency and 7-14 day timeline, recommends monitoring rather than blocking, and specifies they are auto-applied faster than normal upgrades with enhanced monitoring requirements

assertions.expectation: New version available notifications are informational — use for planning, not immediate action
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Default action: No action required - this is informational' for version available notifications and categorizes them as Low urgency with Optional actions focused on planning and testing

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting up Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts for GKE upgrade events
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated section on Pub/Sub integration with specific commands to create topics and subscriptions for GKE cluster events, plus Cloud Logging queries for monitoring and automated notification routing examples

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE release notes as the source for understanding what changed in each version
assertions.reason: Response references GKE release notes multiple times as a source for understanding changes, including checking release notes for relevant changes in version available notifications and reviewing release notes for upcoming versions in the weekly checklist
